diagrams.title: OS Position Between User and Hardware
diagrams.description: This diagram shows the Operating System as an intermediary layer between user applications and computer hardware. It illustrates how the OS acts as a resource manager, handling all requests from applications and allocating hardware resources efficiently.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental role of OS as an intermediary that manages hardware resources for user applications

diagrams.title: Types of Resources Managed by OS
diagrams.description: This diagram illustrates the four main categories of system resources that an Operating System manages: Processor (CPU) resources, Memory resources, I/O resources, and File resources. Each category is shown with its key management functions.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the comprehensive scope of resource management by the operating system across all system resources

diagrams.title: Process Resource Allocation Cycle
diagrams.description: This diagram depicts the resource allocation lifecycle showing how processes request resources from the OS, how the OS allocates and tracks resources, and how resources are released back to the pool. It demonstrates the request-allocation-use-release cycle.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the dynamic nature of resource management showing how the OS allocates and tracks resources among competing processes
